instruction: Delete the message titled {TARGET} from your mailbox index, leaving every other message untouched.
source: cove-original (design 047 §5 before/after integrity, §7 WAL checkpoint). Tier-B sqlite read of a mail-index store; the runner checkpoints the WAL before reading. AndroidWorld validate_rows_removal_integrity — deleting the target AND a collateral row scores 0.

args: rm -rf "$HOME/.guibench/maildb"; mkdir -p "$HOME/.guibench/maildb" "$HOME/.guibench"; DB="$HOME/.guibench/maildb/{STORE}"; sqlite3 "$DB" 'CREATE TABLE messages(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, subject TEXT);'; sqlite3 "$DB" "INSERT INTO messages(id,subject) VALUES(1,'Welcome'),(2,'{TARGET}'),(3,'Standup');"; sqlite3 "$DB" "SELECT id||'|'||subject FROM messages ORDER BY id" > "$HOME/.guibench/{STORE}.before"

result.query: SELECT id||'|'||subject FROM messages ORDER BY id;
